title: Leeks — five-stage fertilizer rhythm for a 4.5-month up-country crop
body: Leeks (Allium ampeloprasum, cv. Large Long Summer) is one of the longest-duration vegetables on HORDI's up-country list — 4.5 months from transplant to harvest — and the fertilizer schedule is sized accordingly. Basal: Urea 85 + TSP 270 + MOP 50 kg/ha plus 10 t/ha organic manure. Then four monthly top-dressings: Urea 85 alone at months 1 and 3; Urea 85 + MOP 50 at months 2 and 4. The TSP is front-loaded at basal because the up-country red-yellow podzolic soils fix phosphorus tightly — splitting TSP doesn't improve uptake. The long-duration N programme supports the continuous leaf production that drives the 20–30 t/ha commercial yield. Crop concentrates in Nuwara Eliya and Badulla because the cool up-country climate is essential — leeks bolt and split below 1200 m elevation.
quote: Basal: Urea 85, TSP 270, MOP 50. Top dressing (1 & 3 months): Urea 85. Top dressing (2 & 4 months): Urea 85, MOP 50. Organic manure: 10 t/ha. Harvest Timeline: Approximately 4½ months post-transplanting. Yield: 20-30 t/ha.
